Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The user has provided 1 A database schema for insurance claim handling with tables claims adjusters claim_events 2 A temporal profile model showing average times between activity pairs 3 Some identified anomalies in that model 4 A prompt asking me to identify anomalies generate hypotheses and propose SQL verification queries

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: My task is to Identify anomalies in the temporal profile Generate hypotheses for why they exist Propose SQL queries to verify these anomalies

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The intended process flow is R A E P N C

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: R to P Average about 25 hours with STDEV of only 1 hour This is suspiciously low variability

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: A low STDEV suggests either The data is artificially constrained Theres a fixed schedule for approvals The sample size is very small Theres some automation forcing a fixed delay

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: P to N Average 7 days with STDEV of 2 days This is extremely long Normally notification should happen quickly after approval

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This suggests Manual notification process with delays Backlog in notification system Batch processing of notifications Resource constraints

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: A to C Average 2 hours with STDEV of 1 hour This is very quick The normal flow should go through Evaluate Approve Notify before Close

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: A 2 hour turnaround suggests Some claims are being closed immediately after assignment without proper evaluation Fast track claims that bypass normal steps Data errors where Close events are recorded incorrectly

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: E to N Average 5 minutes with STDEV of 1 minute This is extremely fast Normally there should be approval between Evaluate and Notify

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This suggests Approval step is being skipped Notification is happening too quickly after evaluation Possible data recording errors Automated system that notifies immediately after evaluation

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: E to C Average 1 hour with STDEV of 50 minutes This is also fast Similar issues as A to C suggests premature closure

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: N to C Average 30 minutes with STDEV of 5 minutes This seems reasonable but combined with the long P to N time it suggests the bottleneck is at notification

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Some pairs are not in the direct sequence but are in the model R to P This should be roughly R A plus A E plus E P A to C Should be A E P N C E to N Should be E P N E to C Should be E P N C

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The anomalies suggest that the actual process does not always follow the intended sequence Some steps might be skipped or there might be parallel paths

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: There might be a business rule that all claims must be approved within exactly 25 hours of receipt causing artificial constraint Or the data might be from a period where approvals were batched at fixed intervals

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Notification might be a manual process done at the end of the week causing the 7 day average Or there might be a separate team that handles notifications with limited capacity

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Some claims might be auto closed if they meet certain criteria for example very small claims Or there might be data errors where Close events are recorded with wrong timestamps

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: The Approve step might be missing from the event log for some claims making E to N appear direct Or notification might be automated to happen immediately after evaluation for some claim types

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Similar to A to C some claims might be closed immediately after evaluation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: I need to write queries that 1 Identify claims with unusual timing between specific activity pairs 2 Correlate with adjusters claim types regions etc 3 Find patterns like claims closed immediately after assignment

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query to find claims where P to N is more than 14 days

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: But this might be too strict with the no other events condition Maybe better to just look at all P to N pairs regardless of intermediate events

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query to find claims closed within 4 hours of assignment and check if there are missing intermediate steps

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query to find claims where E to N is less than 10 minutes and check if Approve is missing

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query to check the distribution of R to P times

Name: Validation - PE
Text: And to find outliers outside 3 SD

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Claims with fast A to C grouped by adjuster

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Claims with long P to N grouped by claim type

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Verification queries should 1 Identify specific claims with extreme timing 2 Check for missing intermediate steps 3 Correlate with claim attributes type amount and resources adjusters
